prompt_summary: P0 data integrity + signal logic: Data Sufficiency Gate (weekly_closes<200 blocks zone)
score_correctness: zone guard on BUY (ELEVATED/EXTREME downgrade to HOLD)
score_completeness: Added DATA SUFFICIENCY GATE after Step 1b; deterministic quorum_verdict truth table before Step 2; BUY* and BUY** rows for ELEVATED/EXTREME zone downgrades; HOLD catches zone=UNKNOWN
column11: All three P0s are directly addressed — the data-sufficiency gate blocks zone classification below 200 weekly closes, the BUY rule now carries an explicit zone guard, and the quorum truth table is exhaustive and deterministic. The biggest gap is an undefined zone taxonomy: the guard references FAIR_VALUE and EXTREME but no thresholds or classifier output the diff shows ever produce those labels, so BUY** may be dead code and any name mismatch silently fails the guard; the diff also never wires the new count=210 timeframe=W weekly fetch into the helper's weekly_closes input, leaving the gate's count and the MA computation on potentially different data.

prompt_summary: P1 guardrails: verdict critic every token (n/total denominator + INCOMPLETE gate); citation validator non-skippable subagent; portfolio governor regime-aware buy cap (F&G buckets)
output_summary: Step 4a enforces all-token critic; Step 4c n/total denominator with INCOMPLETE block; Step 5b non-skippable guard with verbatim output rule; Portfolio Governor table with F&G-based caps and ranked downgrade logic
judge_feedback: P1-1 (n/total denominator) and P1-2 (non-skippable subagent with verbatim raw output) are both clean and directly address their defects. The governor (P1-3) is the weak link: a Fear-regime cap of 6 would NOT have fired on the very 6/11-BUY scenario that motivated it, and capping count of BUY signals is a poor proxy for 60-70% dry-powder capital discipline; plus Greed/euphoria (F&G 50-100) is entirely uncapped where concentrated buying is most dangerous.

prompt_summary: P2 publishing + UX: Telegram 4096-byte split logic (ban head -c 4000); crypto-daily Step 0c 7→11 tokens; X.com Cmd+Enter keyboard fallback documented
output_summary: crypto-advisor Step 6 gets explicit 4096-char split with per-part send and disclaimer on final part only; crypto-daily Step 0c fixed to 11 tokens; Step 4b adds keyboard fallback block after click
judge_feedback: P2-2 is a clean correct count fix (7→11). P2-1 conflates units — states '4096 bytes (hard limit)' then tests 'exceeds 4096 chars' which is exactly the distinction that matters for multibyte emojis; 'split at token boundaries / never mid-token' leaves token undefined for a cold model. P2-3's biggest gap: the comment says 'If click fails use keyboard fallback' but the code runs unconditionally after every click so on a successful post it fires a redundant Cmd+Enter against a closed compose box — the documented behavior and implemented behavior diverge.
